title: Congo's Ebola Outbreak Is Spreading Faster Than the 2014 Epidemic
script: Ebola's breaking records! The Democratic Republic of Congo is fighting an Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus — a species with no vaccine or proven treatment. As of June 8, the Africa CDC reported 550 confirmed cases and 101 confirmed deaths. The pace is historic: the outbreak is spreading faster than the catastrophic 2014 West Africa epidemic in its first weeks. The World Health Organization says eastern Congo's Ituri Province is nearly impossible to contain because armed conflict prevents health workers from reaching patients. Uganda has now reported 19 confirmed cases and 2 deaths. The U.S. CDC projects a 65% chance of cases exceeding 20,000 within 3 months if only 20% of patients are isolated. There is no approved vaccine for Bundibugyo Ebola. Is this the outbreak that finally breaks containment?
